events.title: Saudi Arabia Launched Covert Attacks on Iran
events.desc: Saudi Arabia has reportedly launched numerous unpublicized strikes on Iran in retaliation for attacks within the kingdom during the Middle East war. This marks the first known instance of Saudi Arabia directly carrying out military action on Iranian soil, indicating a bolder approach to self-defense against its regional rival.

events.title: Lebanon Says Israeli Strikes Killed 39
events.desc: Israeli forces have conducted strikes in Lebanon resulting in the deaths of 39 individuals. This escalation follows a period of continued exchanges of fire between Israel and Hezbollah, despite a previously announced ceasefire deal.

events.title: Israeli Military Investigates Soldier's Act Against Virgin Mary Statue
events.desc: The Israeli military is investigating an incident where a soldier was photographed desecrating a Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon. This follows a previous incident involving a Christian figure, raising concerns about religious insensitivity and potential provocation.

events.title: Lebanon Says Two Paramedics Among 13 Killed in Israeli Strikes
events.desc: Israeli strikes in Lebanon have resulted in the deaths of 13 people, including two paramedics. The paramedics were reportedly on a rescue mission in Nabatieh following an earlier attack.

events.title: Hamas 'Weaponised' Sexual Violence in 7 October Attacks, Israeli Investigation Claims
events.desc: An Israeli investigation claims that Hamas systematically 'weaponised' sexual violence during the 7 October attacks. The report details harrowing accounts of sexual assaults, presenting a comprehensive account of the alleged atrocities.

events.title: Israel Passes Law to Allow Death Penalty for 7 October Attackers
events.desc: Israel has enacted a law permitting the death penalty and public trials for individuals involved in the Hamas-led attacks of October 7. This legislation is a significant development in the country's response to the attacks.

events.title: Fears of Renewed Gaza War as Hamas Disarmament Talks Stall
events.desc: Reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting in Gaza due to an impasse in disarmament talks with Hamas. The stalled negotiations raise fears of a renewed conflict in the region.

events.title: Fighting in Sudan’s Blue Nile State Displaces Thousands
events.desc: Fighting in Sudan's Blue Nile State has led to the displacement of thousands of people. Reports from Al Karama camp detail the ongoing humanitarian crisis.

events.title: Exclusive-Iraq, Pakistan Strike Energy Deals with Iran
events.desc: Iraq and Pakistan have entered into energy deals with Iran, including shipments of oil and liquefied natural gas. This development demonstrates Tehran's asserted control over energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Vietnam State Oil Company Urges US Navy to Allow Tanker Through Blockade
events.desc: The trading arm of Vietnam's state oil company has requested the U.S. Navy permit a crude oil tanker, carrying Iraqi oil, to pass through its Middle East Gulf blockade. The tanker is intended to supply a Vietnamese refinery.

events.title: UK to Send Drones, Jets, and Warship to Secure Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The UK will contribute autonomous mine-hunting equipment, Typhoon fighter jets, and the warship HMS Dragon to a multinational mission aimed at securing shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. This initiative is set to become operational when conditions permit.

events.title: Putin Hails Russia’s Test Launch of ‘Most Powerful Missile in the World’
events.desc: Russian President Vladimir Putin announced the successful test launch of the nuclear-capable Sarmat missile, described as the world's most powerful. The missile is slated to enter combat service by the end of the year.

events.title: ‘Snoopy’, ‘Adolf’ and ‘Password’: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their corresponding passwords have been found circulating online. The breach affects 12 out of 13 ministries, potentially exposing sensitive information and highlighting significant security vulnerabilities.

events.title: Mining China’s ‘Little Red Book’ for Open Source Gold
events.desc: This guide details methods for conducting open-source research within China, acknowledging the country's digital restrictions and monitored platforms. It offers strategies for navigating these challenges to gather information.

events.title: The Clandestine Network Smuggling Starlink Tech into Iran
events.desc: A clandestine network is reportedly smuggling Starlink satellite internet terminals into Iran to circumvent internet blackouts and allow citizens access to outside information. This operation aims to provide connectivity and counter government restrictions.

events.title: Bahrain-led UN Resolution on Strait of Hormuz Gains Support
events.desc: A UN resolution initiated by Bahrain, calling for free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and an end to Iranian attacks on neighboring Gulf states, has garnered the support of 112 nations.

events.title: China and US Agree on Opposing Hormuz Tolls
events.desc: Senior US and Chinese officials have agreed that no nation should be permitted to impose shipping tolls in the Strait of Hormuz. This alignment precedes a summit between Presidents Trump and Xi, where Iran's control over the waterway will be a key agenda item.

events.title: US Government's Energy Arm Assumes Strait of Hormuz Will Stay Shut
events.desc: The U.S. Department of Energy's statistical arm anticipates the Strait of Hormuz will remain effectively shut through late May, with traffic resuming gradually thereafter. This projection has led to increased forecasts for U.S. motor fuel prices.

events.title: Mexico, CIA Reject Report of US Assassination Campaign Against Cartels
events.desc: Both Mexico and the CIA have refuted a media report alleging that the CIA has directly participated in deadly anti-cartel operations. The rebuttals come amid ongoing efforts to combat drug cartels in the region.

events.title: Honduras Mayor Arrested for Masterminding Environmentalist’s Killing
events.desc: A mayor in Honduras has been arrested for allegedly masterminding the killing of an environmentalist. The mayor was among three individuals detained in connection with the shooting of activist Juan Lopez.

events.title: Peru Presidential Candidate Charged with Financial Crimes
events.desc: Roberto Sanchez, a presidential candidate in Peru, faces charges of financial crimes. Prosecutors are seeking a five-year and four-month prison sentence for alleged false financial disclosures.

events.title: His Father Had Just Been Buried. Then West Bank Settlers Forced Him to Dig Up the Body
events.desc: The UN human rights office has condemned an incident in the West Bank where settlers allegedly forced an individual to exhume his father's body. This event is described as 'appalling and emblematic of the dehumanisation of Palestinians.'

events.title: Israel Deports Two Activists Detained on Board Gaza Flotilla
events.desc: Israel has deported two activists, Saif Abu Keshek (Spanish national) and Thiago Ávila (Brazilian), who were detained while participating in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla. The deportation follows their detention at sea.

events.title: EU Agrees Sanctions on Israeli Settlers Over West Bank Violence
events.desc: The European Union has agreed to impose sanctions on Israeli settlers responsible for violence in the West Bank. The EU's foreign policy chief stated that 'extremism and violence carry consequences,' a move Israel has termed 'arbitrary.'

events.title: Ailing Iranian Nobel Laureate Given Bail and Hospital Transfer
events.desc: Iranian authorities have granted bail to human rights activist and Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi, facilitating her transfer to a hospital. This decision comes amid growing concerns over her deteriorating health in prison.

events.title: Lebanon Authorities Release Salafist Cleric Khaled Hablas
events.desc: Lebanon has released Salafist cleric Khaled Hablas, who was imprisoned for his role in the 2014 clashes with the military and Hezbollah. His release follows lobbying efforts by Syria for the return of detained militants.

events.title: 'Not My Son's Fault': Women Bearing Children of Sudan's War Rapes
events.desc: Women in Sudan are bearing the children resulting from rape by paramilitary fighters during the ongoing conflict. The article highlights the profound and lasting trauma inflicted upon survivors.

events.title: DRC's Coltan Belt: Verifying Deadly Landslides At Mines Under M23 Control
events.desc: At least four landslides have reportedly killed hundreds of people at the Rubaya mines in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) since the beginning of 2026. These mines are a significant source of coltan and are currently under the control of the M23 group.

events.title: Unearthing a Colombian Politician’s Connections to Neo-Nazi Active Club Group
events.desc: An investigation has uncovered connections between a Colombian political candidate and the neo-Nazi Active Club group. This discovery highlights the presence of extremist ideologies within political circles.

events.title: “Make Iran Ungovernable” – Tracking Efforts to Destroy Iran’s Police Infrastructure
events.desc: Efforts to degrade Iran's regime capacity have targeted its police infrastructure. At least 80 police stations and Basij paramilitary bases have been damaged or destroyed in the initial weeks of the conflict between the United States and Israel against Iran.

events.title: Painkiller Pipeline: 300 Million Tapentadol Pills Sent from India to West Africa
events.desc: An investigation has revealed that Indian companies have shipped over 320 million synthetic opioid pills to West Africa over the past three years, despite the drug not being approved by regulators in the region. This operation is referred to as the 'Painkiller Pipeline.'

events.title: How India’s Ruling Party is Using AI to Boost Hate Speech
events.desc: India's ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is reportedly using artificial intelligence to amplify hate speech, particularly in states bordering Bangladesh. Examples include videos and captions targeting minority groups.

events.title: The War You’re Not Allowed to See: UAE Downplays Iranian Strikes
events.desc: Authorities in the United Arab Emirates have reportedly downplayed the impact of Iranian drone strikes, misrepresented interceptions, and failed to acknowledge successful attacks. This discrepancy between official statements and open-source evidence suggests an effort to control the narrative surrounding the conflict.

events.title: As Adversaries Integrate, U.S. Partners Bypass Washington
events.desc: Drones targeting Gulf Arab states are based on Iranian designs refined by Russia, posing a challenge that the U.S. is struggling to counter. This situation has led some U.S. partners to seek counter-drone assistance from Ukraine.

events.title: Could Russia Follow the “Hormuz Playbook” in the Baltic and Black Seas?
events.desc: The article explores whether Russia might employ tactics similar to those used in the Strait of Hormuz, where drone strikes and insurance market pressures effectively closed the waterway, in the Baltic and Black Seas.

events.title: The Pentagon Needs a Playbook for Munitions Surge Production
events.desc: The U.S. military faces challenges in rapidly replenishing munitions, as demonstrated by the lengthy production times for missiles. The Russo-Ukrainian War offers lessons for accelerating munitions production timelines.

events.title: Ankara’s Crossroads: Rearmament, Risk, and the Prospect of War with Israel
events.desc: Tensions between Israel and Turkey are escalating due to issues related to Syria, the Palestinian territories, and regional security. There are warnings of a potential clash between the two nations, fueled by historical grievances and geopolitical calculations.

events.title: Trump Calls Iran Response to US Proposal to End War 'Totally Unacceptable'
events.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump has described Iran's response to a U.S. proposal for ending the war as 'totally unacceptable' and 'unbelievably weak.' Iran's demands reportedly include the lifting of the U.S. naval blockade and recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Iranian Activist Tells BBC How Fear of War Restarting Intensifies Trauma of Repression
events.desc: A dissident in Tehran shares concerns that the Iranian regime may become more entrenched and seek revenge after the war ends. The fear of the conflict restarting intensifies the psychological pressure and trauma of repression faced by ordinary Iranians.

events.title: Bowen: Strait of Hormuz Standoff Raises Risk of Sliding Back into All-Out War
events.desc: The ongoing standoff in the Strait of Hormuz between the U.S. and Iran, coupled with their determination to maintain pressure, has put the fragile Gulf ceasefire in serious jeopardy, increasing the risk of a return to all-out war.

events.title: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire is on 'Massive Life Support'
events.desc: President Trump has criticized Iran's counteroffer regarding the month-long ceasefire in the war, describing it as 'massively on life support' and 'unbelievably weak.'

events.title: Iran Demands Guarantees for World Cup Participation
events.desc: The Iranian Football Federation (FFIRI) has presented FIFA with 10 conditions for its participation in the World Cup. These demands are presented amidst ongoing international tensions.

events.title: Iran Accuses US of 'Reckless Military Adventure'
events.desc: Iran's representative Abbas Araghchi has accused the United States of engaging in 'reckless military adventure,' stating that U.S. attacks occur whenever diplomatic solutions are being considered.

events.title: Iran War Live: Trump Travels to China as Conflict with Tehran Looms Large
events.desc: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have resulted in a significant number of casualties since March 2. The ongoing conflict and President Trump's travel to China highlight the heightened geopolitical tensions surrounding Iran.

events.title: Trump Says He Doesn't Need Xi's Help on Iran
events.desc: President Donald Trump stated that while he will discuss the war in Iran with Chinese President Xi Jinping during his upcoming visit to China, he does not believe he requires Xi's assistance, asserting the U.S. will achieve victory regardless.

events.title: Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit, Iran Looks to China as Guarantor
events.desc: Tehran is closely monitoring the upcoming Trump-Xi summit to assess whether China will continue to act as a primary protector or will exert pressure on Iran. Iran expresses reservations about China's role.

events.title: New Turkey Maritime Draft Bill Fuels EastMed Tensions
events.desc: Turkey's proposed maritime bill seeks to formalize its claims in the Aegean, Eastern Mediterranean, and Black Seas. This move is increasing tensions with Greece and Cyprus over contested maritime boundaries and offshore energy rights.

events.title: Zelenskyy’s Ex-Chief of Staff Appears in Court in Money-Laundering Case
events.desc: Andriy Yermak, former chief of staff to Ukrainian President Zelenskyy, has appeared in court facing accusations of involvement in an alleged $10.5 million money-laundering scheme related to a luxury real estate development.

events.title: ‘It’s a Failed Nation’: Trump Pressures Cuba as Fuel Crisis Deepens
events.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump has characterized Cuba as a 'failed nation' as his administration intensifies its pressure campaign against the island, coinciding with a deepening fuel crisis.

events.title: Argentines Protest Against Milei’s Defunding of Public Universities
events.desc: Citizens in Argentina are protesting against President Milei's decision to defund public universities, expressing their opposition to the measure's impact on higher education.

events.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Fires Back at Drinking Allegations
events.desc: FBI Director Kash Patel engaged in a heated exchange with Senator Chris Van Hollen during a Senate budget hearing, addressing allegations related to drinking.

events.title: Starmer at Risk Because He Pushed Labour to be ‘New Conservative Party’
events.desc: Author Oliver Eagleton suggests that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's position is at risk due to his perceived shift of the Labour Party towards a more conservative stance.

events.title: Chinese Firm Unveils ‘Transformer’ Style Manned Robot
events.desc: Unitree Robotics has released footage showcasing its CEO piloting the GD01 transformable mecha, a robot capable of smashing through walls in a 'transformer' style.

events.title: Sam Altman Says Elon Musk Wanted 90 Percent of OpenAI
events.desc: Sam Altman revealed that Elon Musk had sought a 90 percent stake in OpenAI during a high-stakes trial that could significantly influence the future leadership and direction of the company.

events.title: Don't Mention the War: Tucson Prepares to Welcome Team Iran
events.desc: Despite the ongoing conflict and U.S. military actions against Iran, Tucson, Arizona, is preparing to host the Iranian national football team for the upcoming World Cup, creating a stark contrast between geopolitical tensions and sporting events.

events.title: UN May Put Cyprus Peace Plan Forward This Year
events.desc: Cyprus's President Nikos Christodoulides stated that the United Nations is likely to initiate a renewed effort to resolve the longstanding division of Cyprus before the end of UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres' term.

events.title: Ronaldo’s Al-Nassr Kept Waiting for Saudi Pro League Title by Own Goal
events.desc: An own goal by goalkeeper Bento in injury time denied Al-Nassr a 1-0 victory against rivals Al Hilal, postponing their chance to win the Saudi Pro League title.

events.title: BBC Unmasks Key People Smuggler in Network Behind Most Small Boat Crossings
events.desc: The BBC has identified a key people smuggler, operating under the alias 'Kardo Ranya,' who has evaded arrest and is believed to be central to the network responsible for most small boat crossings.

events.title: Close the Gap? Italy Sets New Rules for Its First National Security Strategy
events.desc: Italy is poised to publish its first-ever national security strategy, a significant development for the only G7 country lacking one. Recent political consolidation and external pressures have created an environment conducive to its creation.

events.title: Conflict, CASEVAC, and the Golden Hour in the Age of Persistent Surveillance
events.desc: The article examines Western battlefield casualty evacuation doctrine, which is based on assumptions from the 'Global War on Terror.' It discusses the concept of the 'golden hour' and its relevance in the context of persistent surveillance.

events.title: The Army Needs to Build Better Command Posts
events.desc: The article critiques the design and functionality of U.S. Army tactical operation centers (TOCs), referring to them as 'TOC Mahals.' It argues for the need to build more effective and efficient command posts.

events.title: How Much Longer Can Russia Last in the War?
events.desc: This piece revisits an earlier assessment on Russia's capacity to sustain the war in Ukraine, considering factors such as population size, economy, and munitions production after another year of combat.

events.title: The Cop on the Corner Is Our First Line of Defense: Local Police and Surveillance
events.desc: The article highlights how local police officers play a crucial role in detecting potential threats, citing an incident where a routine traffic stop uncovered a terror plot. It emphasizes the importance of local law enforcement in surveillance and counter-terrorism efforts.

events.title: Between Intent and Capability: Assessing Iranian Attacks on the U.S. Homeland
events.desc: Despite threats from Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps to deploy capabilities against the U.S. homeland, a comprehensive assessment is needed to understand the gap between intent and actual capability. U.S. authorities have been on high alert.

events.title: Sensationalism Doesn’t Serve Society
events.desc: This piece is part of 'The Ukraine Compass,' a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary. It aims to provide a broader perspective on Ukrainian issues beyond just the battlefield, capturing the texture of daily life, politics, and public debate.

events.title: What Does SOUTHCOM’s New Autonomous Warfare Command Herald?
events.desc: General Frank Donovan discusses the vision behind SOUTHCOM's new Autonomous Warfare Command, focusing on integrating drones and autonomous systems for strategic effects like disrupting cartel networks and enhancing allied cooperation.

events.title: The Other Border Problem: Russia and China’s Lawfare Threaten the Arctic
events.desc: The Arctic is becoming a contested space due to Russian and Chinese lawfare, including excessive maritime regulations and challenges to Western claims. Their converging tactics aim to exert influence and challenge freedom of navigation.

events.title: Does OPEC Still Matter?
events.desc: The United Arab Emirates has announced its withdrawal from OPEC, effective May 1, ending its nearly six-decade membership. This decision, significant in terms of oil production, comes amid heightened global attention on oil prices due to the war with Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: What 300 Emails Say About Americans and the Army’s Direct Commission Program
events.desc: An analysis of over 300 inquiries for the Army's Direct Commission Program reveals insights into the motivations of professionals seeking to serve and the program's effectiveness in connecting them with Army branches.
